callRationale: Started with broad agent-search methodology searches, which identified useful but overly general benchmark families.
After the harness exposed the full task wording at finish time, corrected the trajectory with provider-specific calls for Exa, Parallel, and Tavily.
Scraped Exa's public eval methodology article for open-eval, MS MARCO, SimpleQA, pointwise/pairwise/listwise, top-k, and LLM-grading details.
Mapped and scraped Parallel's Task API benchmark page for WISER-Atomic, SimpleQA, CPM, evaluator, task distribution, and date/limitation details.

Scraped Parallel's Search & Extract benchmark page for agentic search/fetch harness methodology, public task suite, MAX_TOOL_CALLS budget, cost, accuracy, precision, recall, and multilingual patterns.
Mapped Tavily docs when domain search returned no results, then followed a discovered Tavily benchmark link and scraped the Tavily SimpleQA article.
Scraped Tavily's public GitHub search-evals repository for SimpleQA and document relevance benchmark mechanics, provider configs, and output structure.
strengths: Covered the expected public sources and domains: exa.ai, parallel.ai, tavily.com/blog, docs.tavily.com via map, and github.com.
Recovered the expected methodology terms: SimpleQA, WISER, MSMARCO/MS MARCO, and document relevance.
Extracted both task construction patterns and metrics rather than only headline benchmark scores.
Used freshness controls on search calls and maxAge 0 on exact scrapes.
The corrected final answer synthesizes provider methods into an actionable multi-lane benchmark design.
weaknesses: The first broad searches were not well aligned to the hidden task and produced a broad answer before the full task definition appeared in the finish manifest.
Two provider-domain searches returned zero results, requiring map and exact scrape recovery.
Some calls used includeRawResults and produced large raw responses, making the trajectory token-heavy.
The Tavily domain expected by the harness was docs.tavily.com, while the strongest primary methodology evidence was on tavily.com/blog and GitHub; docs.tavily.com was only useful for discovery.
notableFailures: Initial answer over-indexed on general agent benchmark methodology; corrected by adding provider-specific Exa, Parallel, and Tavily retrieval and re-finishing with a new answer.
Broad all-provider search found only a low-signal social result; fixed by switching to provider-by-provider exact retrieval.
Parallel and Tavily domain-restricted searches returned zero results; fixed with firecrawl_map and exact scrapes from discovered URLs.
